preamble.summary: Moses's birth during Pharaoh's genocide. He is placed in a basket, found by Pharaoh's daughter Tharmuth, raised in the court, flees to Midian after killing an Egyptian.
preamble.remarkable: Jubilees provides jubilee dating for Moses's birth. Pharaoh's daughter is named Tharmuth — unnamed in Exodus.

verses.text_reference: And in the forty-eighth jubilee thou wast born.
verses.rendering: In the fourth week, in the sixth year of the forty-eighth jubilee, you were born. This was the time of tribulation for the children of Israel.
translator_notes: The angel addresses Moses directly — the book's frame narrative.

verses.text_reference: And Pharaoh commanded that all male children be cast into the river.
verses.rendering: Pharaoh king of Egypt decreed: every male child born to the Israelites was to be thrown into the river.

verses.text_reference: And thy mother hid thee for three months.
verses.rendering: They carried out the drownings for seven months, until you were born. Your mother hid you for three months.

verses.text_reference: And she made an ark and placed thee in the reeds.
verses.rendering: She made an ark, coated it with pitch and tar, placed it among the reeds on the riverbank, and set you in it for seven days.

verses.text_reference: And the daughter of Pharaoh, Tharmuth, found thee.
verses.rendering: Pharaoh's daughter Tharmuth came to bathe in the river. She heard you crying and told her attendants to fetch you.
translator_notes: The name 'Tharmuth' is unique to Jubilees. Josephus calls her Thermuthis.

verses.text_reference: And she had compassion on thee.
verses.rendering: She lifted you out of the ark and felt compassion for you.

verses.text_reference: And thy sister said: 'Shall I find a Hebrew nurse?'
verses.rendering: Your sister said: Shall I find a Hebrew woman to nurse this child? She said: Go.

verses.text_reference: And she called thy mother Jochebed.
verses.rendering: She went and called your mother Jochebed. She was paid wages, and she nursed you.

verses.text_reference: And thou wast brought to the daughter of Pharaoh.
verses.rendering: When you were grown, they brought you to Pharaoh's daughter. You became her son. Your father Amram taught you to write.
translator_notes: Amram teaching Moses to write emphasizes the Levitical literary tradition.

verses.text_reference: And thou wast at court until thou didst slay the Egyptian.
verses.rendering: You were at court for three weeks of years until you saw an Egyptian beating an Israelite. You struck the Egyptian dead and buried him.

verses.text_reference: And on the next day two Israelites were fighting.
verses.rendering: The next day you found two Israelites fighting. One said: Who made you ruler over us? Do you plan to kill me as you killed the Egyptian? You fled.

verses.text_reference: And thou didst flee to Midian and married Zipporah.
verses.rendering: You fled to Midian, lived with Jethro the priest, married his daughter Zipporah, and she bore you sons.
